title: Flaky Citrusy Uruguayan Fish Empanadas
description: These empanadas combine the flaky crust of traditional Uruguayan empanadas with the brightness of citrus and the savory flavor of fish, making for a cozy and delicious pescatarian-friendly dish. The citrus and salt balance perfectly to bring out the flavors of the fish.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine flour and salt. Add cold butter and use a pastry blender or fingers to work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add cold water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
In a skillet, heat a couple of tablespoons of oil over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add cod pieces to the skillet and cook until they start to flake, about 3-4 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
Deglaze the skillet with white wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Stir in orange and lemon juice.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch. Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon or two of the fish mixture onto the center of each dough circle. Brush the edges with the beaten egg for a golden glaze and fold the dough in half, pressing edges to seal.
Place empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each empanada. Brush tops with the remaining egg wash.
Bake empanadas in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
